metadata.title: The pH-Triggered Lock That Lets RNA Drugs Escape the Cell's Trash
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Cancer Nanomedicine candidate 05. Source chapter: cancer-nanomedicine/chapters/09-nucleic-acid-and-gene-delivery.md (the ionizable lipid / endosomal escape mechanism only).
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on newsprint ground; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines; pH charge-flip morph as the key graphic moment
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, ink near-black body text, teal = escaped RNA / ionizable lipid working / cytosol; crimson = trapped RNA / endosomal degradation; gold = the pH switch moment

metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = escaped RNA / ionizable lipid working / cytosol; CRIMSON = trapped RNA / endosomal degradation; GOLD = the pH switch
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: four LNP components tour, siRNA vs mRNA vs CRISPR cargo comparison, Onpattro/COVID-vaccine history, and viral vectors. One mechanism: the charge switch that opens the trash bag.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Pragmatist register (voices/pragmatist/VOICE.md + HAI.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the HAI outro from AUTHOR.MD :: Humanitarians AI
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: The siRNA silenced its target 90 percent in the dish. In the mouse, near zero effect. Tumors kept growing.

card.copy: The pH-Triggered Lock That Lets RNA Drugs Escape the Cell's Trash
card.sub: why a drug that works in a dish fails in a body

beats.new_visual_element: [TITLE] the siRNA paradox — dish vs mouse

beats.narration_text: Months were spent evaluating alternative targets. The failure was misdiagnosed. When they tracked where the siRNA went, it never reached the cytosol. The cell packaged it into an acidic membrane bubble and digested it before it could act.

props.headline: The team spent months looking for a better target. But they…

beats.scene_description: a cell cross-section diagram showing siRNA (small dots) trapped inside an endosome (acidic orange bubble), with a lysosome nearby labeled 'digested'; cytosol visible but unreached; newsprint editorial collage style
beats.image_prompt: B02, schematic cross-section of a eukaryotic cell interior, small siRNA molecules trapped inside a circular endosome vesicle with a pH 5.5 label, endosome shown as acidic bubble in warm tones, lysosome nearby, cytosol as open space unreached, flat editorial diagram pinned to aged newsprint, desaturated except for the endosome highlight, no text overlay
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL] siRNA trapped in endosome — the wrong-failure reveal

beats.narration_text: A lipid nanoparticle delivers siRNA into cells. The cell immediately seals it in an acidic vesicle and digests it. The siRNA that works in culture does nothing in vivo. How does the ionizable lipid solve this?

card.copy: How does the ionizable lipid solve the endosomal trap?
card.sub: the gap formula: siRNA works in a dish but not in a mouse — why?

beats.narration_text: After injection, the nanoparticle is taken up via endocytosis: the cell wraps membrane around the particle and pinches off a sealed vesicle — the endosome. That is the default fate. Most cargo never leaves it.

production_viz.label: LNP enters cell → endosome forms
production_viz.mechanic: a circle representing an LNP moves toward a cell membrane (arc); the arc wraps around the LNP circle and pinches off a new enclosed circle — the endosome; label 'endosome' appears beside it
production_viz.note: crimson = the trap — the endosome is the problem, not the solution

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] endocytosis — LNP wrapped into endosome bubble

beats.narration_text: Proton pumps drive hydrogen ions into the endosome, dropping pH from 7.4 to approximately 5.5. That acidification primes the contents for digestion. For most cargo, digestion is the outcome.

production_viz.label: pH drops 7.4 to 5.5 inside the endosome
production_viz.mechanic: endosome circle sits on screen; a label shows pH 7.4 then morphs/transforms to pH 5.5 with color shift from neutral to acidic; small H+ labels appear inside the circle
production_viz.note: gold for the pH switch moment — the key transition

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] pH drop from 7.4 to 5.5 — the acidification step

beats.narration_text: The ionizable lipid's head group carries an amine — a nitrogen that picks up or releases a proton. At blood pH 7.4 the amine is neutral; the particle circulates without adhering to proteins or cell surfaces. At pH 5.5 inside the endosome, the amine protonates and gains a positive charge.

production_viz.label: ionizable lipid: neutral at 7.4 -> cationic at 5.5
production_viz.mechanic: a lipid head group (circle) sits against a pH 7.4 label — neutral, INK color. pH label transforms to 5.5 (GOLD sweep). The head group morphs/transforms to show a '+' label (TEAL). This is the key morph move.
production_viz.note: THE key scene — head group flips from neutral to positive as pH drops; use Transform not animate chaining

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] the charge flip — neutral at 7.4, cationic at 5.5

beats.narration_text: Now cationic: the positively charged ionizable lipids interact electrostatically with anionic lipids in the endosomal membrane. Opposite charges attract, destabilize the bilayer, and the membrane ruptures. RNA cargo releases into the cytosol — where the RNA interference machinery operates.

production_viz.label: cationic lipid disrupts endosomal membrane — RNA escapes
production_viz.mechanic: the endosome circle (CRIMSON outline) has a gap cut into it — lines removed from the arc — and small teal dots (RNA) stream outward through the gap into the surrounding cytosol space
production_viz.note: the crack: use actual shape removal (FadeOut of arc segment), not animate; teal dots stream out = escaped RNA

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] membrane crack — RNA escapes to cytosol (TEAL dots)

beats.narration_text: Only one to two percent of internalized cargo escapes. That sounds like failure. It is enough — that thin stream reaches the RNA interference machinery intact and is sufficient for gene silencing. It is what separates a functional LNP from an inert liposome carrying identical cargo.

production_viz.label: ~1-2% escape rate — still enough to act
production_viz.mechanic: a narrow funnel shape: wide at top (100 teal dots entering), narrow at bottom (2-3 teal dots escaping); label '~1-2% escapes' beside the narrow neck; annotation 'rate-limiting step'
production_viz.note: funnel visual: most crimson (trapped), tiny teal stream (escaped)

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] escape funnel — 1-2% gets out, enough to act

beats.narration_text: Two formulations, identical siRNA. LNP-A with conventional neutral lipid: eight percent silencing at 48 hours — most RNA digested in lysosomes. LNP-B with ionizable lipid: eighty-four percent silencing. Same cell, same cargo, one charge-switching component.

production_viz.label: LNP-A (neutral) vs LNP-B (ionizable) — 8% vs 84% silencing
production_viz.mechanic: two side-by-side columns: left = LNP-A labeled NEUTRAL LIPID, a small crimson bar labeled '8%'; right = LNP-B labeled IONIZABLE LIPID, a tall teal bar labeled '84%'; subtitle 'illustrative' beneath both
production_viz.note: illustrative example from the card — label as illustrative; no specific drug names

beats.narration_text: Without the pH-responsive charge switch, the particle enters the endosome and cargo is digested. The ionizable lipid is the functional component — a pH-sensitive lock that only opens at endosomal pH.

document.quote: A vehicle with a pH-sensitive lock that only opens inside the trash bag.

beats.new_visual_element: [QUOTE] pH-sensitive lock quote card with gold highlight

beats.narration_text: The endosome is the rate-limiting step. The ionizable lipid's charge flip — neutral in blood, cationic inside the endosome — opens that step. That is what converts an inert liposome into a functional RNA therapeutic.

card.copy: Neutral in blood. Cationic in the endosome. That charge flip is the drug.

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] endcard — the charge flip is the drug

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: This is part of the Cancer Nanomedicine series from Humanitarians AI.

props.tagline: AI education for humanitarian and social-impact practitioners.
